experiences.intentions: Current piece is J in rotation 1, sitting in the upper-middle with open air below. Surface is uneven but there is a clear vertical channel around columns 5-6; immediate threats are low because stack height is modest. Goal is to place the J efficiently without creating overhangs, ideally moving toward a cleaner fit for upcoming I/T/L pieces. Candidate actions: left/right shift the J before committing; rotate_right to rotation 2 makes a flat 3-wide bar with a foot on the right, useful for shelfing but not yet aligned; soft/hard drop would commit prematurely at current column. Moving right best aligns the J with the open center-right well so a later drop/rotation can tuck it more cleanly and preserve flexibility.
